agent_output: {"domain_analysis":{"business_context":"Subscription billing platform managing lifecycle states (activation, renewal, cancellation) with payment validation, retry logic, tier-specific grace periods, and free trial constraints.","bounded_context":"Subscription Billing","ubiquitous_language":[{"term":"Subscription","definition":"A recurring billing agreement between a customer and the platform, progressing through states: trial, active, renewing, grace period, cancelled."},{"term":"Payment Method","definition":"A stored instrument (card, bank account) used to collect subscription fees; has a validity/failure status."},{"term":"Billing Cycle","definition":"The recurring period (e.g., monthly, annual) that defines when payment is due."},{"term":"Renewal","definition":"The automated process of charging the subscriber's payment method to extend service for another billing cycle."},{"term":"Renewal Attempt","definition":"A single try to collect payment during the renewal window; up to 3 attempts are made for standard tiers."},{"term":"Grace Period","definition":"A 14-day window granted to Premium tier subscribers after payment failure, during which the subscription remains active before cancellation."},{"term":"Free Trial","definition":"A time-limited subscription that provides service at no cost; limited to one active trial per email address."},{"term":"Premium Tier","definition":"A subscription level that receives enhanced cancellation protection via a 14-day grace period instead of the 3-strike rule."},{"term":"Cancelled Subscription","definition":"A terminal subscription state from which reactivation is impossible; the customer must create a new subscription."}]},"business_rules":[{"id":"BR-1","rule":"A subscription cannot be activated with a failed payment method on file.","invariant":true,"invariant_justification":"This is a state integrity constraint. Activating a subscription against a known-bad payment method creates a corrupt state — the system would promise service it cannot collect revenue for. This is a data consistency gate, not a tunable policy threshold.","testable_assertion":"ASSERT that any attempt to activate a subscription where the associated payment method has status 'failed' is rejected and the subscription does not transition to 'active'."},{"id":"BR-2","rule":"Subscriptions auto-renew 7 days before the billing cycle ends to allow retry time.","invariant":false,"invariant_justification":"The 7-day lead time is a business policy designed to give adequate retry runway. A VP could change this to 5 or 10 days based on payment processor SLAs or retry analytics without corrupting system state.","testable_assertion":"ASSERT that the renewal process is triggered exactly 7 days before the billing cycle end date."},{"id":"BR-3","rule":"After 3 failed renewal attempts, the subscription is automatically cancelled.","invariant":false,"invariant_justification":"The 3-attempt threshold is a configurable business policy. The business could decide to allow 2 or 5 retries based on churn analysis. The system is not corrupt at 2 or 4 failures — this is a policy knob, not a consistency constraint.","testable_assertion":"ASSERT that when a non-Premium subscription accumulates 3 consecutive failed renewal attempts, it transitions to 'cancelled' state automatically."},{"id":"BR-4","rule":"A cancelled subscription cannot be reactivated — customers must create a new subscription.","invariant":true,"invariant_justification":"This is a state machine integrity constraint. 'Cancelled' is a terminal state. Allowing reactivation from 'cancelled' would break the directed acyclic lifecycle, create ambiguous audit trails (is this the same subscription or a new one?), and corrupt billing history and reporting. The business explicitly mandates a new subscription entity.","testable_assertion":"ASSERT that no API call, user action, or system process can transition a subscription from 'cancelled' to 'active'. The only path to service is creating a new subscription entity."},{"id":"BR-5","rule":"Premium tier subscribers get a 14-day grace period before cancellation instead of the 3-strike rule.","invariant":false,"invariant_justification":"The 14-day duration and the decision to use a grace period model (vs. retry-count model) for Premium are business-tier policies. The business could adjust the grace period to 10 or 21 days, or change which tiers receive this treatment, without violating system integrity.","testable_assertion":"ASSERT that when a Premium tier subscription fails renewal, it enters a 14-day grace period instead of incrementing a failure counter, and is cancelled only after the 14-day window expires without successful payment."},{"id":"BR-6","rule":"Free trial subscriptions cannot have more than one active trial per email address at any time.","invariant":true,"invariant_justification":"This is a uniqueness/data integrity constraint. Allowing multiple concurrent trials per email would corrupt the business model — it enables abuse (infinite free service) and makes trial-to-paid conversion tracking unreliable. Like a unique constraint on a database, violating it produces a fundamentally inconsistent state.","testable_assertion":"ASSERT that any attempt to create a free trial subscription for an email address that already has an active trial is rejected, and the existing trial remains unchanged."}],"acceptance_criteria":[{"id":"AC-1","given":"A customer has a payment method on file with status 'failed'","when":"The customer attempts to activate a subscription","then":"The activation is rejected with a clear error indicating the payment method must be valid before activation"},{"id":"AC-2","given":"A subscription is active with a billing cycle ending on date X","when":"The current date reaches X minus 7 days","then":"The system initiates an automatic renewal charge against the subscriber's payment method"},{"id":"AC-3","given":"A standard (non-Premium) subscription has failed renewal 2 times","when":"The 3rd renewal attempt also fails","then":"The subscription is automatically transitioned to 'cancelled' and the customer is notified"},{"id":"AC-4","given":"A subscription is in 'cancelled' state","when":"Any actor attempts to reactivate it","then":"The request is rejected; the system directs the customer to create a new subscription"},{"id":"AC-5","given":"A Premium tier subscription fails its renewal attempt","when":"The failure is detected","then":"The subscription enters a 14-day grace period with service maintained, and is cancelled only if no successful payment occurs within those 14 days"},{"id":"AC-6","given":"An email address already has an active free trial subscription","when":"A request is made to create another free trial for the same email","then":"The request is rejected with an error indicating only one active trial per email is permitted"},{"id":"AC-7","given":"A Premium subscription is in its 14-day grace period","when":"A successful payment is processed within the 14-day window","then":"The subscription returns to active state and the grace period is cleared"}],"edge_cases":[{"scenario":"Payment method fails between renewal trigger and charge processing","expected_behavior":"The renewal attempt should check payment method validity at charge time, not just at trigger time. If failed, it counts as a failed renewal attempt."},{"scenario":"Customer updates payment method to a valid one during the retry/grace window","expected_behavior":"The next renewal attempt should use the updated payment method. If successful, subscription remains active."},{"scenario":"Premium subscriber downgrades to standard tier during the 14-day grace period","expected_behavior":"Business must decide: does the grace period persist (honor the tier at time of failure) or does the 3-strike rule take over? This needs clarification."},{"scenario":"Free trial expires while customer also has a paid subscription on the same email","expected_behavior":"The one-trial-per-email rule applies only to active trials. A paid subscription and an expired trial do not block a future trial (if business allows re-trials)."},{"scenario":"All 3 renewal attempts fail on the same day (e.g., system processes retries rapidly)","expected_behavior":"The system should space retry attempts to give customers time to fix payment issues. Immediate triple-failure defeats the purpose of the 7-day runway."},{"scenario":"Customer has two email addresses linked to the same account","expected_behavior":"The one-trial-per-email constraint must clarify: is it per email address literally, or per customer/account? If per email, a customer with two emails could get two trials."}],"business_impact":{"financial":"Correct enforcement of payment validation at activation (BR-1) prevents revenue leakage from unpayable subscriptions. The 3-strike and grace period rules balance churn reduction against carrying non-paying subscribers. Trial abuse prevention protects conversion funnel economics.","operational":"Auto-renewal 7 days early reduces payment failure noise by spreading retries. Terminal cancellation state simplifies subscription lifecycle management and eliminates ambiguous reactivation edge cases. Grace periods for Premium require monitoring and notification infrastructure.","user":"Premium grace periods improve retention for high-value customers. The no-reactivation rule creates friction but ensures clean billing history. Trial uniqueness may frustrate users who want to re-trial after a gap.","risk":"The largest risk is the interaction between Premium grace periods and the standard 3-strike rule — tier changes during failure states are undefined. Trial enforcement by email alone is gameable with email aliases (user+tag@domain.com).","stakeholders_affected":[{"group":"Customers (Standard tier)","impact":"Subject to hard 3-strike cancellation; must maintain valid payment methods proactively"},{"group":"Customers (Premium tier)","impact":"14-day grace period provides significantly more recovery time, improving experience"},{"group":"Finance/Revenue team","impact":"Terminal cancellation state and activation gating ensure clean revenue recognition and reduce bad-debt write-offs"},{"group":"Customer Support","impact":"No-reactivation rule will generate support tickets; support must be empowered to help customers create new subscriptions smoothly"},{"group":"Growth/Marketing team","impact":"One-trial-per-email constraint limits trial abuse but also limits re-engagement strategies using repeat trials"}]},"confidence":{"level":72,"reasoning":"The six rules are explicitly stated and internally consistent. Classification confidence is high. Score is below 80 because several edge cases (tier changes during grace, retry spacing, email alias handling, re-trial eligibility after expiry) are not addressed in the spec, and the Premium grace period interaction with the standard retry model has ambiguity.","high_confidence_areas":["Invariant classification for all 6 rules","Terminal state semantics of cancelled subscriptions","Payment method validation as activation gate","Trial uniqueness as integrity constraint"],"low_confidence_areas":["Behavior when Premium subscriber downgrades during grace period","Whether retry attempts must be spaced or can fire in rapid succession","Email alias handling for trial uniqueness (user+tag@domain.com)","Whether a customer who completed a trial can ever start another one later"],"assumptions":["Premium tier grace period completely replaces the 3-strike model (they are mutually exclusive, not additive)","One-trial-per-email means concurrent active trials, not lifetime limit","The 7-day renewal window applies to all tiers equally","Failed payment method status is a stored, queryable attribute on the payment method entity"]},"escalations":[],"rejection_reasons":[]}
agent_output_excerpt: {"domain_analysis":{"business_context":"Subscription billing platform managing lifecycle states (activation, renewal, cancellation) with payment validation, retry logic, tier-specific grace periods, and free trial constraints.","bounded_context":"Subscription Billing","ubiquitous_language":[{"term":"Subscription","definition":"A recurring billing agreement between a customer and the platform, progressing through states: trial, active, renewing, grace period, cancelled."},{"term":"Payment Method"
